subject: Databases
title: SQL Is Declarative
description: A quick, practical guide to SQL Is Declarative.

hook.text: In Python, you write loops to filter data. In SQL, you say what you want and the database figures out how.

buildup.text: Declarative means you describe the result, not the steps. SELECT name WHERE age > 18 — no loops needed.

discovery.text: The query planner decides the execution strategy: which index to use, what order to join tables.

twist.text: This means two identical queries can run very differently depending on the data and indexes available.

climax.text: Declarative SQL lets beginners write correct queries. Performance tuning comes later with experience.

punchline.text: Tell SQL what you want. It decides how to get it.

quiz.question: What does it mean that SQL is declarative?
options: You describe the result you want, not the step-by-step procedure to get it
You must declare all variables before using them
SQL only works with declared schemas
